headline: PE's 2026 Rebound: Better Deals Coming, But Make Your Move Count

stories.title: Trinity Hunt Closes Berger Consulting Investment as PE Dives Into Services
stories.summary: Trinity Hunt Partners just backed Berger Consulting Group in March 2026, launching a whole economic consulting platform. Lowkey shows PE's appetite for professional services and the buy-and-build playbook that's heating up.
stories.takeaway: PE is moving into consulting and services with purpose—if you're in this space, firms are actively looking to consolidate and scale.

stories.title: Healthcare M&A Set for Breakout Year—AI-Enabled Services Are Hot
stories.summary: Healthcare deal value bounced hard in Q4 2025 ($22B), and PE is pivoting hard toward AI-based revenue cycle management, telehealth platforms, and medtech innovation. Medtech carve-outs and take-privates are gonna be massive.
stories.takeaway: Healthcare's where the money's flowing in 2026—especially if you can spot AI-enabled efficiency plays that strip costs.

stories.title: Stop Aging Out of PE—Network Like Your Career Depends On It
stories.summary: On-cycle recruiting for PE analyst roles starts crazy early (12-18 months before start dates), and consultants have a narrower window than bankers. The key: you gotta have concrete PE experience from your consulting work or you're dead in the water.
stories.takeaway: If you're in consulting and want PE, don't wait. Get secondments to PE clients, build your network NOW, and position yourself in the 1-2 year sweet spot before you 'age out.'

watchNext.title: Private Equity Outlook 2026: Dry Powder Deployment & Continuation Vehicles
watchNext.why: Deep dive on how PE firms are actually deploying capital, managing the backlog of unsold companies, and why continuation funds are becoming the new normal. Critical for understanding what deals are actually getting done.

watchNext.title: 2026 Healthcare Investment Themes: Alternative Structures & Creative Deals
watchNext.why: PwC's breakdown of why traditional buyouts are stuck but creative deal structures (carve-outs, minority investments, take-privates) are exploding in healthcare. This is how PE wins when valuations don't cooperate.
